title: Censored Laps-To-Pit Survival Model
summary: This target is naturally a discrete event process: each row is a state with a remaining time-to-next-stop, and many final stints are right-censored by the finish.
rationale: This target is naturally a discrete event process: each row is a state with a remaining time-to-next-stop, and many final stints are right-censored by the finish. Public survival work with time-varying covariates suggests hazard/AFT models can use the same tabular features but learn event timing more directly than plain binary classification.
implementation_hint: Create a secondary target `laps_until_next_pit` within each driver-race sequence, right-censor rows that never pit again, and train a survival model (`CatBoost SurvivalAft` or `XGBoost survival:aft`) with grouped OOF by race-year. Convert predicted remaining-life to `P(stop in 1 lap)` or blend its rank with the current classifier.
expected_effect: Better ordering on ambiguous late-stint rows and cleaner handling of finish-censored negatives; likely complementary to the current classifier.
risk: Needs leakage-safe forward construction of next-stop labels within each race sequence; poor probability conversion from survival score to next-lap risk can erase gains.
